mnemonics: CHAT-T: Cholera, Hepatitis A, Amoebiasis, Typhoid, Tetanus - Water-related diseases
keyPoints: Water-borne diseases are caused by ingestion of contaminated water (e.g., cholera, typhoid).
Water-washed diseases are caused by poor hygiene due to water scarcity (e.g., trachoma, scabies).
Water-based diseases are caused by parasites that spend part of their life cycle in water (e.g., schistosomiasis).
commonMistakes: Confusing water-borne (ingested) with water-based (skin contact) disease transmission.
Assuming all water-related illnesses are caused by drinking contaminated water, ignoring water-washed diseases.
Overlooking that tetanus, while not water-borne, is water-induced and associated with flooding.
